title: Whats the name of this clothing
display_title: Whats the name of this clothing
wikitext:
[]: What's the name of this clothing? Post by Draconis » Tue Jul 12, 2011 7:53 pm Was just wondering what the name of the clothing Asuka is wearing in this scene is. SPOILER: Show

[]: Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Tue Jul 12, 2011 8:48 pm I did a quick Wikipedia perusal of "dress types," and the closest match seems to be a jumper (American definition) or a sundress . Since Japan is likely to be hot and humid at the time of the story, I think it's likely that Asuka is wearing a sundress.

[]: Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Jul 12, 2011 9:22 pm I'm no fashionista, nor am I obsessed with women's clothing unlike the pantsu or clothing fetish adherents (for me, it's always what's underneath that pantsu and top that counts, LOL) but IIRC, that is a nightgown known better as a babydoll

[]: Post by Draconis » Tue Jul 12, 2011 9:45 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: but IIRC, that is a nightgown known better as a babydoll

[]: Post by symbv » Tue Jul 12, 2011 9:48 pm In Japan it is simply called a "one-piece skirt home-wear" ワンピーススカート部屋着 This is close to what Asuka wore: [URL=http://min.us/lbRl4k] [/URL]

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Jul 12, 2011 10:03 pm symbv wrote: In Japan it is simply called a "one-piece skirt home-wear" ワンピーススカート部屋着

[]: Post by symbv » Tue Jul 12, 2011 10:09 pm It is more "simple" as the lack of specific term like babydoll or sundress. It is just a combination of the general functional words of fashion. Actually given the obsession with fashion in Japan I would have expected more specific name for such dress. Perhaps I was looking at the wrong place -- it is not like otakus in Japan know a lot about fashion

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Jul 12, 2011 10:18 pm symbv wrote: It is more "simple" as the lack of specific term like babydoll or sundress.

[]: Post by symbv » Tue Jul 12, 2011 10:26 pm Otaku can give long complex and imaginative name for mecha and weapon, but fashion is definitely not their forte Actually I just checked some fashion retail sites. It seems that it indeed has no special name for such wear. Here is a dress that looks similar to Asuka's style and it is also named "one-piece skirt room wear". So it is not only the otakus.... Also the wording as understood in Japan can be confusing. For example Here the dress is referred as "camisole one-piece" while in the west camisole does not extend beyond the waist. Also found that babydoll now carries erotic and sexual connotation and so some refer to the dress as "shortie nightgown".

[]: Post by Draconis » Tue Jul 12, 2011 11:24 pm Well, isn't babydoll the equivalent name regardless? They look exactly like "one-piece skirt home-wear," but with a simpler, more American name. And even if the name DOES carry a sexual/erotic connotation, it'd make all the more sense that Asuka's wearing one. How does the connotation lead to the conclusion to also call it a "shortie nightgown"? Do people just call it that instead to ignore the stigma or what? Edit: Disregard that last line. Just read it on the Wiki.

[]: Post by symbv » Tue Jul 12, 2011 11:35 pm Draconis wrote: Well, isn't babydoll the equivalent name regardless? They look exactly like "one-piece skirt home-wear," but with a simpler, more American name.

[]: Post by Draconis » Tue Jul 12, 2011 11:43 pm symbv wrote: Well I also read it in wiki - Quote: "Styles of the same general length but not intended to emphasize sex appeal are sometimes called shortie nightgowns." Not sure how it came about though.

[]: Re: What's the name of this clothing? Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jul 13, 2011 10:56 am Draconis wrote: Was just wondering what the name of the clothing Asuka is wearing in this scene is. SPOILER: Show

[]: Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jul 13, 2011 11:33 am It's not called a "fuck me slip"? I mean it's SOOOO easy to take off. One motion & BOOM you're ready to go. Though watching 2.0 makes me wonder if Asuka has ever watched "Anchorman"? If it's so hot in Japan why is she drinking milk? It seems.... A BAD CHOICE.

[]: Re: What's the name of this clothing? Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 13, 2011 11:38 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: ...And everyone who posted in this thread is male.

[]: Post by Shogo-Kun » Wed Jul 13, 2011 12:23 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: It's not called a "fuck me slip"? I mean it's SOOOO easy to take off. One motion & BOOM you're ready to go.

[]: Post by Fireball » Wed Jul 13, 2011 1:28 pm Oh, you mean tit curtains?

[]: Post by Ornette » Wed Jul 13, 2011 2:32 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: It's not called a "fuck me slip"? I mean it's SOOOO easy to take off. One motion & BOOM you're ready to go.

[]: Post by Draconis » Wed Jul 13, 2011 4:15 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: It's not called a "fuck me slip"? I mean it's SOOOO easy to take off. One motion & BOOM you're ready to go.

[]: Post by symbv » Wed Jul 13, 2011 9:12 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Though watching 2.0 makes me wonder if Asuka has ever watched "Anchorman"? If it's so hot in Japan why is she drinking milk? It seems.... A BAD CHOICE.
